prediction.reasoning: Based on historical volatility patterns for large-cap healthcare stocks like Medtronic (MDT), I applied a geometric Brownian motion model with estimated annualized volatility of ~25%. For a 20-day horizon, this translates to daily volatility of ~1.6%. The point estimate assumes slight upward drift typical of established dividend-paying healthcare companies. Confidence intervals widen progressively to reflect increasing uncertainty over the 20-day period, with the 90% interval spanning approximately ±16% from current price.
